ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Lala zugu ka ya wulisiri ya mansi halibeoo, ni, ya badima daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban daa ku anabidima maa.
translated: The elders of the community have gathered to discuss the troubles of the land, and, it seems the youth are also present to listen to their wise words.

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Tinsi dima nin yisigi n‑tuusi taaba tɔppu, ni nadima gba nin tuusi taaba. Kɔm nin ti lu, ka tingbaŋŋu maa nin damni kpa, ka nin damni kpa;
translated: The darkness was complete, there was no moon, the stars were not shining, and the night was dark. The wind was blowing, it was making a lot of noise, and it was very strong;

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: amaa ŋɔn sa'am Sii Kasi yuuri maa, u ku nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi halli saha kan ka naari, ka u nin di la saha din ka tariga taalli.”
translated: “My mother used to tell me that if you eat Kasi porridge, you will see a good dream that night, and you will have a good day the next day.”

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man dinni, man gba nin ti zaani m Ba tooni arizana ni, n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔn nyɛ la n dinni.
translated: And Jesus said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath. Therefore, the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: N ziim nla. N ziim wa nin ti yi na, ka di yiibu na nin suŋŋi niriba pam ka kyɛ ka nangbanyinni zaalipaalli ti zaani n‑tiri ba, tumbeeri taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi zugu.
translated: They are very happy. They are happy because they have eaten and drunk and have enjoyed themselves very much, and their stomachs are now full; they are now satisfied and are lying down.

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Tɔ, ka tiisi maa wala saha ŋɔn daa ti pɔri, u daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani nti deaai u puu maa diibu na n‑ti u.
translated: When he was still a boy, he used to go to his farm with his father's farmhands to help them and learn how to farm.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘N bii nŋɔa, mani n yiisi u: N wuri u, ka n sufu laa m‑pɛligi u zugu. N nin zaŋŋi n Sii Kasi n‑niŋi u ni, U nin wulisi n sariya karibu yɛla N‑ti tinsi zaa niriba maa.
translated: The chief said, 'My child, I will tell you: I gave birth to you, I breastfed you, and I did not abandon you. I took you to my Sii Kasi and made you there, and it blessed my whole family and gave us wealth.'

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa daa zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem, ka laa n‑yɛli arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa mari ninvugseeba maa yɛla.
translated: The chiefs and elders of the community gathered to deliberate on the strange occurrence and to find out who was behind the unusual and frightening incident, and they said to the wise men that they should find out what was happening.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Ban gyɛrigi la daa mari ba firiladima maa, amaa ba daa bu niŋi kpaam ga'a ni m‑mari m‑paasi.
translated: They had already performed the customary rites for him, but they had not yet organized a big feast for him.

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Ka ban daa wum Naaba ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la, ba daa yi n‑lu Bɛtlihɛm soori yaasa, ka saŋmariwuu maa daa gaari ba tooni ka ba daa dɔla - ban daa pun nya seaa wuntampusiri zii la - halli nti paai n‑zaani saazugu n‑wulisiri bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.
translated: When they had heard the king, they departed; and, behold, the star which they had seen in the east went before them, till it came and stood over where the young child was. When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy; and when they were come into the house, they saw the young child with Mary his mother, and fell down, and worshipped him: and when they had opened their treasures, they presented unto him gifts; gold, and frankincense, and myrrh.

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Ka zama pam daa dɔli u, ka u daa tibri ba bɛni.
translated: When I was a child, I used to follow him, and he used to give me food.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, ba daa kyɛ na nti zaŋŋi kum maa m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai.
translated: The elders of the community gathered and discussed the matter, they agreed to go and take the girl to be given to the chief to marry.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: Ni, “Arizana naam laa n‑ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- walima daansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa goori m‑boori kugsuma, bumpeela nyain, an ligri daa zoaai pam.
translated: He said, “My name is Arizana, I am from Nŋmanni. I came here to learn, and I have been here for a while. I have been able to learn farming, hunting, gathering, and I have been able to catch fish with a fishing hook.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Karimbii yi ti paai u karimba tataabu, di nin sakki u ni. Ka daabri yi paai u dugma saani gba, di sakkiya. Ba yi zaŋŋi Arizimbeeri Naa yuuri m‑pa yiriseaa yiridaana, ni, Biyɛlizibuli n nyɛ u, ba bu ya booli u yiri maa dima lala? - halli ni yuseesi an bɛ n‑gaari lala.”
translated: When they realized that the drum was the one that was making the noise, they beat it. When the drumbeat reached the ears of the chief, he got angry. He asked, “Is this Arizimbeeri Naa who is making so much noise; is Biyɛlizibuli the one who does not obey the elders, why does he make so much noise?” - “I do not know who told you.”

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Ka u dugma daa yɛli u, ni, ‘Mboo! Daabsuŋŋu ni yɛdda daana n nyɛ i gba! Nyin ŋɔn su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi n‑zɛ yimmu maa, n nin zaŋŋi bunkara fukumsi n‑ti i ka i mara. Dizugu kyɛm na ti di n nya'asim maa.’
translated: He called out to him, saying, ‘Friend! The big basket that you have, take it down. You have been speaking harshly to me, I will take revenge on you. I will give you a beating you will never forget.’

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni,
translated: And Jesus said to them again,

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Di daa bu yuui ka tingbaŋŋu daa vibgi pam, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi arizana ni n‑sigi na n‑vuui gballi dunoori kugbiliŋŋu maa m‑biligi ka n‑yi kpaŋŋa n‑zinni ka zugu.
translated: Today I have come to tell you good news, and I, Dugma Malaaka, have come to give you a joyful message that will make your heart happy and your mouth smile.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Din daa paasi beela, ban daa zɛ bɛni maa daa laa n‑yɛli Piita, ni, “I ku toaai n‑kyiisi, ni, i pa ba nyaandɔla, dama i gba tɔ'asiri la Galiliitiri ka ti baŋŋi i yɛla di zugu.”
translated: One day passing by, they came to a place and he said to Peter, “I am hungry, I have not eaten; go and catch a fish in the lake of Galilee and cook it on the fire so that we may eat.”

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Ban daa paai Kapirinyum tiŋŋa sahanseaa, ninvugseeba daa kyɛ ba saani na ni ba ti toosi Gyɛrusalɛm Gyeeŋŋu Yiri lampoo ligri maa. Ba daa yɛli Piita, ni, “Ammi, ya karimba bu yoori ti lampoo la?”
translated: One day, as they were going up to Jerusalem, Jesus was walking ahead of them. They were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. They took aside the twelve and told them what was going to happen to him, saying, “Listen, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the scribes; they will condemn him to death, and then hand him over to the Gentiles; they will mock him, spit on him, flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again.” Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to him and said, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Ŋɔn daa yi la'aŋŋu duu maa, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑ti kpɛ Saimoon ni Anduru yiri ni, ŋɔna ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.
translated: The man was very happy and he was glad that he had met Saimoon and Anduru at their house, with Gyeemsi and Gyɔni.

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Tɔ; ka lala saha maa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.
translated: It; if you want to live well with Saddusidima - people say, if you want to live long - then you must respect and honour her.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, Zaadaari halli Sɔdoom ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.”
translated: My mother said to me, "If you go to Zaadaari, the people of Sodoom will give you trouble on the way."

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi, ka ba daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.
translated: If you do not listen to your mother, she will not be happy and will pray that you may die young.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.
translated: The elders used to say that a person who has a bad character will still show it even if you give them a good upbringing; even if you give them a good upbringing and a good name, their bad character will still be evident.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Ka ba yi daa bu ŋmaai dabsa maa n‑gyiŋŋi, saalibii n daa ka ni n‑da naa n‑nya tiliginsim, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi seeba la zugu ka ba siri daa ŋmaai dabsa maa.
translated: When they were about to sleep, a strong smell came out from it, but the man did not know what it was, until the next morning, and behold, the smell had become very strong.
